name: Grilled Potatoes with Garlic
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb small red new potatoes, scrubbed
recipeInstructions:
Prepare grill or heat oven to 450°F. Make a tray from heavy-duty aluminum foil by folding foil over for double strength and folding up the sides; oil tray. Cut potatoes into pieces no larger than 1 1/2 inches.
Combine the potatoes, garlic, 1 tbsp olive oil, and 1/4 tsp salt in a medium-sized bowl; toss to coat potatoes. Spoon the potato mixture into the foil tray, keeping potatoes in one layer. Top with rosemary sprigs.
To grill: Grill the potato mixture 5 inches above medium-hot coals, covered, turning occasionally, for 25-30 minutes or until potatoes are tender and crisply browned.
To roast: Roast the potato mixture in a 13x9x2-inch baking pan in a 450°F oven, stirring occasionally, for 30-40 minutes or until tender and browned.
Meanwhile, whisk vinegar, mustard, remaining 1/4 tsp salt, and pepper in a medium-sized bowl. Whisk in remaining 3 tbsp oil. Discard woody rosemary stems from the tray. Squeeze garlic cloves from skins into dressing in the bowl. Mash slightly into dressing.
Add potatoes and onions to dressing; toss to coat. Serve warm.
